$schema: Canonical API surface for the 4 facts-layer methods + the calculator, shared across packages/ts, packages/py, packages/rs. Per-language casing rules: method and field names are declared here in canonical camelCase; ts uses camelCase as-is; py and rs use snake_case (mechanically: camelCase -> snake_case, e.g. tokenUri -> token_uri, topWitnessShare -> top_witness_share). Each package's api-manifest test asserts its actual exported surface matches this file (mapped through its casing rule), not the other way around -- this file is the source of truth a new language's surface must match to ship (see conformance/README.md).

_notes.FeedbackEntry: This is the facts-layer FeedbackEntry (getAgentFeedback's element type), distinct from the calculator's input FeedbackEntry ({client, score} only, see resultFields._calculatorInput).
_notes.ValidationEntry.method: One of validationMethods below (canonical lowercase strings, not casing-mapped).
_notes.rs.FeedbackEntry (facts.rs): the Rust facts-layer FeedbackEntry currently omits `uri`/`timestamp` (both always null/None in ts/py) -- a documented, harmless surface trim, not a parity gap; the calculator's FeedbackEntry (calculator.rs) is unaffected.
